title: Add Integrated Admin Panel for Site Content Management

requirements.text: Create a backend actor with persistent storage for all manageable site content: founder info (name, title, bio, photo URL), portfolio projects (title, description, category, image, results), services (title, description, icon), and contact form submissions (name, email, message, timestamp). Expose update and query functions for each entity, and a simple admin authentication check using a hardcoded password ('Nikhil@123').
acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores founder, project, service, and contact submission data in stable variables.
Query functions return current data for all entities.
Update functions persist changes for founder info, projects, and services.
Admin login function validates against the hardcoded password and returns a success/failure flag.
Contact submissions can be read from the admin panel.

requirements.text: Add a React-based admin panel accessible at the '/admin' route within the existing single-page app. The panel must include a login screen (username: admin, password: Nikhil@123) that gates access to the dashboard. The dashboard must have sections for: (1) editing founder info including photo URL upload, (2) managing portfolio projects (add/edit/delete), (3) managing services (add/edit/delete), and (4) viewing contact form submissions. All changes must call the backend actor methods and reflect immediately on the public-facing site.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /admin shows a login form.
Incorrect credentials show an error message; correct credentials grant access to the dashboard.
Dashboard has clearly labeled tabs or sidebar sections: Founder, Projects, Services, Contact Submissions.
Founder section allows editing name, title, bio, and photo URL; save updates the backend.
Projects section lists all projects with edit and delete buttons; add new project form is present.
Services section lists all services with edit and delete buttons; add new service form is present.
Contact Submissions section lists all received messages with sender name, email, message, and timestamp.
Public-facing sections (TeamSection, PortfolioSection, ServicesSection) read data from the backend and reflect admin changes.

requirements.text: Update the public-facing TeamSection, PortfolioSection, ServicesSection, and CTASection components to fetch their content from the backend actor instead of using hardcoded static data, so that admin panel changes are reflected live on the website.
acceptanceCriteria: TeamSection displays founder info (name, title, bio, photo) fetched from the backend.
PortfolioSection displays projects fetched from the backend.
ServicesSection displays services fetched from the backend.
Contact form submissions in CTASection are stored via a backend call.
Loading states are shown while data is being fetched.

constraints: All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo.
Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Admin authentication must use the hardcoded credentials (username: admin, password: Nikhil@123).
Admin panel must be a route (/admin) within the existing SPA, not a separate deployment.
nonGoals: Internet Identity or wallet-based authentication for the admin panel.
Role-based access control or multiple admin users.
File/image binary upload to the backend — photo management uses URLs only.
Supabase, Firebase, or any external database integration.
